text.title: Funnel Basics Framework Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Funnel Basics Framework` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The next optimization step MUST be the transition losing the most absolute users, not the lowest-conversion-rate transition. Lowest-conversion-rate prioritisation is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Easy-step optimisation captures small wins; biggest-absolute-loss prioritisation captures the order-of-magnitude wins.

rule.statement: Each funnel step MUST have a documented analytics event name with stakeholder sign-off BEFORE any optimization decision. Mid-flight renames are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Ambiguous step names cause tracking inconsistencies; pinning events upstream prevents diagnosis from data-rot.

rule.statement: Measurement MUST cover ≥ 30 days AND ≥ 100 users per step. Decisions on smaller windows are flagged as hypothesis-only.
rule.rationale: Short windows miss weekly patterns; small samples mislead diagnosis.

rule.statement: Every change MUST be drafted as IF/THEN/BECAUSE: 'IF we change X, THEN metric Y improves by Z%, BECAUSE diagnosis-based reason'. Hand-wave changes are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Un-grounded hypotheses fail at 70%; IF/THEN/BECAUSE forces grounding in diagnosis.

rule.statement: Hypotheses MUST be ICE-scored (Impact / Confidence / Ease) before testing. Highest-ICE goes first. Random / vibes-based prioritisation is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without scoring, teams test what's easy; ICE-anchored prioritisation captures the high-leverage tests first.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
